notes.anchor: Your kinsman is enlarging the city
notes.note: The "kinsman" is Caesar, here mocked for his grandiose scheme to enlarge Rome; Atticus, like Caesar, belonged to plebeian families that claimed remote common descent. Cicero's tone is contemptuous of the upstart's pretensions.
M. Terentius Varro, the great polymath and antiquarian, to whom Cicero dedicated the Academica; the two were warily negotiating the dedication in this period.
M. Iunius Brutus, the future tyrannicide and dedicatee of Cicero's De Finibus; Cicero takes pleasure in reports of Brutus's regard for him, relayed by Atticus.
